tldr: Move geocoding workloads off Google Maps Platform to OpenCage when you want lower cost, GDPR-friendly hosting, and OSM-based data — but keep in mind OpenCage replaces only the geocoding piece, not maps/Places/Directions.
why_switch: Generally lower cost for high-volume geocoding.
OSM-aggregated data with permissive use terms.
Lower lock-in: no Google Cloud account or billing project required.
what_to_export: List of address strings currently geocoded via Google
Cached Google geocoding results (lat/lng + place_id usage)
Code paths calling Geocoding API / Places API for address resolution
API key restrictions and referrer rules
Quota / billing history for sizing on OpenCage
Logging / audit trails tied to Google requests
export_method: Google Maps Platform geocoding results live in your own systems (Google does not provide a bulk export of past requests). Pull cached results from your application database, and pull request volume from Google Cloud Console billing/usage exports to size the OpenCage plan. Check Google's official docs for current usage-export options.
import_method: OpenCage is stateless — there is no import. Update your code to call OpenCage's forward/reverse geocoding endpoint with your OpenCage API key and re-geocode addresses on demand or backfill in batches. Refer to OpenCage's official docs for current endpoint URLs, parameters, and rate limits.

concept_mapping.note: OpenCage has no Google place identifier; rework anything keyed off place_id.
OpenCage is geocoding only; pick a different tool for autocomplete.
Pair OpenCage with a separate map renderer.

gotchas: OpenCage does not replace Maps JS, Directions, Places Autocomplete, or Street View — scope carefully.
place_id-based logic must be redesigned; OpenCage has no equivalent.
Result accuracy varies by region — sample-test critical markets.
Rate limits on lower OpenCage tiers can bite during backfills.
Update key handling: OpenCage keys are server-side, not domain-restricted like Google Maps JS keys.
Re-check attribution and licensing obligations under OSM/OpenCage terms.

email.subject: Moving geocoding from Google Maps Platform to OpenCage
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we move our geocoding workload from Google Maps Platform to OpenCage. Reasons: lower cost at our volume, GDPR-friendly hosting, and a simpler REST API with less lock-in.

Important scoping note: OpenCage replaces forward/reverse geocoding only. Anything we use Google for beyond that — Maps JS, Directions, Places Autocomplete, place_id lookups — stays on Google or moves to a different replacement.

Plan:
1. Inventory every Google geocoding call site and any place_id dependencies.
2. Pull billing/usage data to size the OpenCage plan.
3. Swap forward/reverse calls to OpenCage with caching + rate limiting.
4. Spot-check accuracy in our key markets.
5. Rotate keys and remove unused Google APIs.

Estimated effort: 1-3 days.

Thanks
